sections.title: Starting the quest
Talking to Veliaf Hurtz

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well I'd say our mission to establish a new hideout has been a great success. All thanks to you, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. How's things?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Very good. I'd say our mission to establish a new hideout has been a great success. All thanks to you, of course.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: With the hideout sorted, we can now start focusing on the vampyres again. We're looking to re-establish contact with the Sanguinesti region.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I also want to look into a diplomatic mission into Misthalin. The support of the king would be very helpful in our fight against Drakan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there something I can do to help out?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well, we could use some help with scouting a route into the Sanguinesti region. Are you interested in learning more details?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. Please tell me more.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: There should be another group of Myreque working in the Sanguinesti region. We need to re-establish contact with them. To do that, we need a route into the region.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get into the Sanguinesti region?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The entire region is surrounded by a wall that you'll find impossible to get over.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: That said, you might be able to find a way around the wall at the point where it meets the sea. Perhaps there's something in Burgh de Rott that could help you.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Once you're in, try and establish contact with the Myreque there. I'm sure they'll have sympathisers amongst the citizens. Someone should be able to point you in the right direction.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: One last thing to be aware of is the vyrewatch. They're powerful vampyres that patrol the entire region. We haven't found any way of killing them. Even the Rod of Ivandis does nothing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about the Sanguinesti region?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: It's a large region to the east of here. The southern part is a city called Meiyerditch. Humans live there in ghettos and are regularly tithed by the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The northern part is the vampyre capital of Darkmeyer. It is from his castle there that Lowerniel Drakan rules over all of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get into the Sanguinesti region?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The entire region is surrounded by a wall that you'll find impossible to get over.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: That said, you might be able to find a way around the wall at the point where it meets the sea. Perhaps there's something in Burgh de Rott that could help you.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: One last thing to be aware of is the vyrewatch. They're powerful vampyres that patrol the entire region. We haven't found any way of killing them. Even the Rod of Ivandis does nothing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about the Sanguinesti Myreque?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Certainly. The Myreque, or at least the resistance that has now become the Myreque, originated in the region. Previously there was nothing.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: It was many years ago that a small group, led by a man called Safalaan Hallow, managed to escape from the ghettos of Meiyerditch.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: They got information out about what was happening and helped to organise the resistance, which today is called the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The name was given to us by the locals, who named us after the place where we hid in Mort Myre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, no thanks.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Very well. We'll be here if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will you do here in Burgh de Rott?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Oh, the usual. Keep a low profile and keep an eye on what the vampyres are up to. There's also plenty of research to do with all the new information you've found for us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why re-establish contact with the Sanguinesti region?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The region is where the heaviest blood tithing takes place. It's also home to some of the more senior members of vampyre society, including Lowerniel Drakan himself.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Assuming the vampyres haven't put an end to them, there should be another group of Myreque operating in the region. We will need their support in the fight against the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why ask Misthalin for some help?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: We can't hope to fight the power of the vampyres alone. We'll need help from Misthalin by the bucket-load if we're to stand a chance against them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: I want a word with you!

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Stand where you are!

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Halt! Halt, I say!

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Slurp! You look positively delicious - let me help you lose a bit of weight! Either I get a bite or you can be my combat practice; which is it to be, meat sack?

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Hmm, you look like a tasty specimen - in fact I'm quite peckish right now. You can either give me a bite or I'll use you for combat practice; what do you say?

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Ooh, a meat sack... I could just do with a snack. What's it to be: do I use you for combat practice or do I get a bite?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, have a bite.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give me your best shot, you evil bloodsucker!

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Impressive. Not many of you have the spine for it these days!

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: What? What? Where?

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Ha! I'm not falling for that! Prepare to be tithed...

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Hey, food sack, what's the rush? I was talking to you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey again. I've made my way into the Sanguinesti region, I think!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Good news. Presumably you took precautions not to be followed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... Well anyway, I have some questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to do again?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Now that you've made your way into the Sanguinesti region, we need you to try and make contact with the Myreque there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How should I do that?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You may need to ask some of the citizens, but try to be discreet! Remember to avoid the vyrewatch at all costs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any tips you can give to help out a bit?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You may get lost while in the city. It might be worth your while to invest in some teleportation devices to get yourself out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to a Meiyerditch citizen

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: He-he-hello?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, I wondered if I could have a chat.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Well, I guess so.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Argghghhh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, don't be scared, I just want to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Well, I guess so.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Leave me alone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry but I need to ask a couple of questions.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Well, I guess so.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Get off me! I haven't got anything!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, calm down, I'm not going to hurt you. I just want to ask a few questions.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Well, I guess so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: I'm barely surviving...

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: When I'm not being drained of blood, the vyrewatch force me to repair the walls.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: When I'm not being drained of blood I'm fixing up the ghetto.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: I just try to make a living the best way I can. You haven't got some food have you?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Like everyone else, I just survive here. Just try to keep alive till after the next blood tithe. That's when they feed us.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Why do you wanna know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just curious, that's all.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: I dunno. I don't know if I can trust you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm lost... Can you help me?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Well, you're in sector one, but if you were hoping for any other help then I'm very sorry. I find it hard enough to help myself.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Life's so very hard here. Before too long they'll be wanting to have another blood tithing and then I'll feel just awful again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a blood tithing?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: You must be new here, else you'd know for sure. It's where we have to give a blood tithe to the vampyres. They do it on a weekly basis, a different sector each week so they don't kill us all off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know about the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: It's probably best not to talk about that, friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to make contact with them. Please don't worry - I'm on their side.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: As are we all, but I really can't talk about them. It's not a subject easily broached here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know about the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Yes... but I really can't talk about them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about the Sanguinesti region?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Yep, that's where we are at the moment. This place, Meiyerditch, is just one large ghetto.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: The city is sectioned off so that the vampyres can collect blood tithes on a rotational basis. That way they don't kill us all off in one go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about the vampyres?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: They're monsters, I know that much. I've never known anything else, but I have a feeling it might be nice to not have to live in fear the whole time. Worrying if I'm going to make the next blood tithe quota.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: I know that the juveniles are the lowest form of the vampyres. Apparently they were human once, converted by the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Then you have juvinates. They're initiated juveniles who've had some training and are starting to get some powers.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: After that you have the vyres. They're nasty... really nasty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about Drakan?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Drakan? Which one: Ranis or Vanescula?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I was referring to the main Drakan, you know, the overlord of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Hmm, nothing has been seen of him for many years now. Some people say they occasionally see Ranis or Vanescula flying about, but it's not too often.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is there to do around here?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: To do? Around here? Survive! Eke out an existence and just live for another day, that's it really.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: The juvinates organise work crews to mine the daeyalt, fix the walls, the castles and so on. If we refuse to do the work, they put us on double tithes and you can't do that for long before you dry up.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: If you want to escape, there's only one way. Anyone who survives in the theatre gets to leave. It's pointless though. No one survives.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: It's very nice of you to offer, my friend, but I shouldn't expect that you could do anything. The vampyres are just too powerful to overcome.

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: They say this place once had a Saradominist Queen. Even she could not withstand the vampyres. Anyone who has challenged their power in the past has come undone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to a Meiyerditch citizen after quietly asking about the Myreque

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I really need to meet the Myreque. If you can't talk to me about them, maybe you know someone who will?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: I understand, but you may not like what you find. Go and talk to Old Man Ral. He might help you. He lives in the centre of sector one.

sections.title: Talking to a Meiyerditch citizen after insisting to meet the Myreque

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you introduce me to Old Man Ral?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: Well, I don't need to do that. Just tell him that a friend has recommended you to him. It makes him feel important.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can Old Man Ral help me?

lines.speaker: Meiyerditch citizen
lines.text: He should be able to give you some background information on this place and perhaps put you in touch with people of the same way of thinking as yourself... Hope that helps. See you around.

sections.title: Talking to a Meiyerditch citizen after learning about Old Man Ral
Talking to Trader Sven
Talking to Old Man Ral

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm lost... Can you help me?

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! What do I know about anything? I know nothing of you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything about the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! A simple man only knows simple truths, and what I know is my own business. I share that which is precious only to those who consider it valuable. Nothing will I share with those who are unknown to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just want to help, what should I do?

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! I am a simple man, what little do I know? In esteem, must a man be held, before he feels his efforts are truly appreciated?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're answering every question with a question!

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! Am I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Someone said you could help me.

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! People say things they do not mean - many times this has been true.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's true. They said that you were very well informed. In fact they had a name for you!

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! Oh yes, what was it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Old Man Ral, he's a very good pal.

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! Nope, that's not me they're talking about, must be someone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm sure they gave you a name, but it might have been something else.

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! Okay then, what was it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Old Man Ral, the nefarious rascal.

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! Nope, doesn't sound like me, you sure you've not got me confused with someone else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm pretty sure they directed me to you. What was that name they used to describe you?

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! Yes indeed, what was the name?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Old Man Ral, the Meiyerditch snitch.

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! A snitch! Are you trying to insult me? Get out! Go on!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, no, sorry, I do apologise. I'm sure that was someone else. My mistake entirely!

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! So no one actually referred you to me at all then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No they did, and they referred to you as...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Old Man Ral, the sage of Sanguinesti.

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! Sage of Sanguinesti, eh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, yes, saying you were the local font of knowledge.

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph! So that's what they call me now is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, yes. All the time. And only in the most respectful way.

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmmm. Sage of Sanguinesti. Has a nice ring to it. Yes, that sounds like they were referring to me. What else did they say about me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They said that you might be able to help me find some people... Some hidden people...

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmmmph. That depends. Did they hide themselves or did someone else hide them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, hopefully they hid themselves. I need to make contact with the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph. Myreque, eh? Well, it'll take a lot to find them. It's not an easy route up to sector three, but that's where they are. You'll need to follow the symbols in order to get there.

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: And now you should leave. I have little tolerance for people and talking to you has tired me out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks very much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry to bother you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry to bother you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey. You're with the Myreque aren't you?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Who are you? What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm [player name]. I'm a friend of the Myreque. I've come to help!

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Oh, and I'm just supposed to believe you am I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's true. I was sent by Veliaf. We've just moved base from the Hollows to Burgh de Rott.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Veliaf? Is he still alive? Amazing! Well, you make a convincing story, so I guess I'll have to believe you, for now.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: So anyway, I'm Vertida, one of the Myreque here. What brings you to this miserable place?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was asked to make contact with the Myreque here.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: >Well, it seems like you've done that! I'll tell you what I'll do. I'll write you out a message to give to Veliaf as proof that the Sanguinesti order of the Myreque still exists.

lines.text: There you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: That's really up to you. That said, I imagine you'll probably want to get back to where you came from and deliver that message I gave you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Veliaf.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met, friend. What news?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have met with the Sanguinesti Myreque!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Really? I can't believe it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! They gave me a message to give to you but I don't have it on me at the moment.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well, please bring it to me when you can. I'd be very interested to hear what they have to say.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf Hurtz with the message from Vertida Sefalatis

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Veliaf.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met, friend. What news?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a message for you from the Sanguinesti Myreque!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Great! Please let me see it.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Hmm, sounds like they're struggling just as much as we are. At least they still live though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I need some time to consider how we can best support the Sanguinesti Myreque. In the meantime, I have a new task for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I've had word from Drezel that there's something strange going on at Paterdomus. Head up there and see what support you can offer.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Once you're done, press onwards to Varrock and seek an audience with King Roald. Express the desperate nature of the situation here and beg him to send troops.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Well, I've been with them for years now. We have multiple groups in Morytania but I only really know about those of us based here.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.text: I'm a bit lost... Can you help?

sections.title: Talking to King Roald

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must speak with you on a matter of grave importance.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I'm very sorry, but I'm currently rather busy. Apparently old Drezel is having a bit of bother near Paterdomus and I have to try and sort someone out to go and visit him.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I don't suppose you would fancy popping in and seeing him, would you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, Veliaf asked me to pay him a visit, actually. I guess I could go and meet with him.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: That would be very good indeed. Your king commends you!

sections.title: Talking to Aeonisig Raispher
Talking to Drezel

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Drezel. Veliaf sent me. Apparently there have been some strange goings on which you need me to investigate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, that's a bit odd isn't it? Because in Morytania, strange is the order of the day. I'd have been more surprised if there had been reports of things getting back to normal!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I'm glad you're keeping your sense of humour. It will help you to maintain mental clarity while dealing with the tasks you've been given.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well there's no shortage of tasks for me. Once I'm done here I have to go and speak to King Roald, or rather, Roald. We've been on first name terms for quite a while now. Funny that, eh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To think, a lowly adventurer like me having friends like you and the king. Still, at least I haven't lost my common touch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But anyway, what do you need from me?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Ivan mentioned that he heard some strange noises recently out in front of the temple. Could you have a look and make sure there's nothing funny going on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When and where were these noises heard?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Ivan said he heard them throughout the day, but usually when things were very quiet. We both looked around near the temple but we didn't find anything.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: We were both a bit nervous about going any further west than the steps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think the noises could have come from beyond the steps? I mean, from further west?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: It's certainly possible and more than likely. I'd be grateful if you could extend your search as wide as you can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you hear the noises as well?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I must confess, I have heard some strange things around here recently. Like hushed voices.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, maybe Ivan was whispering to you?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: They didn't sound human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right! That's different! What did they sound like?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Very gruff. almost animal-like in fact.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, never fear, [player name] is here! I'll work this out Drezel, don't you worry!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Has anyone seen or reported anything unusual?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Unfortunately, they have. Several people have gone missing from the local limestone mining area.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Other locals have reported seeing shadowy figures in the Silvaria pass. Could just be an enterprising, though perhaps ruthless, bunch of brigands. If it is, simply give them a taste of Misthalin law and order.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And if it turns out to be more than that?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well then, my friend, I will pray that Saradomin's blessings be upon you and that he guides your actions and keeps you safe from harm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How incredibly reassuring!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Don't take that tone with me! Saradomin's powers are not to be underestimated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How is Ivan? Is he okay?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: He's as well as can be expected considering the circumstances. He's a very confused young man at the moment what with the shadowy events that started his life.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Both his parents died in the Sanguinesti region, though he still holds hope that they may be found alive.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The death of Sani has really affected him as well. Like him, she was an orphan. They had a lot in common.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's all so tragic. I wish there was something I could do for him.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well, you brought him here, which was very brave. He's very grateful to you. It's remarkable, really. He has this inner calmness that I find so very affecting.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: His studies go well and he has Saradomin's blessings, it's clear to see. I would see him as a worthy successor as protector of Paterdomus. When all of his teachings are done, naturally.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, will do.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Ivan mentioned that he heard some strange noises recently out in front of the temple. Could you have a look and make sure there's nothing funny going on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, will do.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf Hurtz after encountering the werewolves

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You've returned already?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. And I have some news!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was attacked near the temple! On the other side of the Salve!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Attacked by who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: By beasts! From Morytania, I believe. Perhaps werewolves.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The vampyres must be behind this! Did you speak with Roald yet? We need his support if we are to end this suffering.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, I'm afraid. I thought you would want to know about the werewolves as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: It's good of you to return with this information, but we need to know Roald's answer before we can make any further plans.

sections.title: Talking to Drezel after encountering the werewolves
Talking to Drezel again

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Hello, [playername]. Have you been to Varrock yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, sorry. What should I do again?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: You need to warn King Roald about possible incursions into Misthalin from Morytania.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, quite right. Thanks for reminding me.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Hello, [playername]. Did you find anything out nearby?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps a bit too much!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: How do you mean, [playername]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was attacked from behind!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Did you see the assailants?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not clearly, but I believe they could have been werewolves.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: This is terrible, terrible news! They have found a way to get across the Salve!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know, it's very worrying.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I must make immediate preparations and meet with other members of my order. You must hurry directly to King Roald and impart this information to him.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Here, take these runes. They should help you to return to Varrock faster.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, I'll make my way to Varrock and bid you good luck.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Likewise, [playername].

sections.title: Talking to King Roald after encountering the werewolves

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must speak with you on a matter of grave importance.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Certainly. What seems to be the problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've witnessed a great many terrible things happening in Morytania, some of which could be happening in Misthalin before long!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: This sounds pretty grave, please go on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have been helping a collection of brave freedom fighters called the Myreque for some time now. They've been fighting against the vampyres and their ruler, Lowerniel Drakan.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: We're all too aware of Drakan and his vampyres. What risk do they pose to Misthalin though?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In Morytania, Drakan and his kin farm humans for their blood! People, my lord, like your own loyal subjects!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not just the citizens of Morytania at risk though. It seems that werewolves have been kidnapping people from this side of the Salve to be used as blood tithes for the vampyres!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: This is awful! Yes, yes of course, we must do something!

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: I would beg my lord not to give in so easily to his very compassionate nature. We know not if these people are loyal Saradomin supporters, my liege.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Oh yes, we need to consider that as well. You're right of course, Aeonisig. Do you know if these people follow Saradomin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am not certain, but surely this isn't the issue? They are in dire need...

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: As are many, my young friend. Is the king meant to leave Misthalin unguarded on the whim of some errant adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you don't understand! It's not just about the people of Morytania. Like I say, there were werewolves in Misthalin!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I dare say you gave fight and slaughtered the foul beasts where they stood!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sad to say that I did not. I had no chance. I was knocked unconscious before I could properly react. Please, Misthalin must come to the aid of the people of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Myreque cannot fight this battle against the vampyres by themselves!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, yes! I will ready my army and prepare to enter Morytania via Paterdomus. Morytania has for too long threatened the borders of Misthalin.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: My lord, this course of action will lead to very severe consequences! Your interference in Morytania will likely upset the balance and could send the world into another cataclysmic God War.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: But Drakan has invaded Misthalin! I must respond!

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: There is no proof that Drakan has invaded Misthalin, my lord!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes there is! I saw it with my own eyes!

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Being knocked out by agents unknown does not constitute evidence. In fact, it's something that the most inexperienced of local folk could accomplish!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: But I can't just stand here and do nothing, Aeonisig! I must try to help if I can.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: I am afraid with the Guthixian edicts regarding religious balance, there is little else we can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guthixian edicts?

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Indeed. They were put in place by Guthix himself at the end of the God Wars. At least, so his followers claim. All of us must abide by them, lest the world fall into ruin again.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: What a load of tosh!

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: An apt description, my lord. The edicts are likely to be little more than propaganda created by the followers of Guthix.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Well then what's the issue?

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: The edicts need closer study before we can be sure.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: But that will take ages, we need to act now!

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: As you say, it will take some time. We now pay the price for our lack of foresight in letting Guthixian edicts restrict the might of Saradomin's will.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, yes, but we can start to make amends for that now, can't we?

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Yes sire, there are some papers to sign, but essentially we can start work on this right away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Signing papers! Is that the sum of the aid that Misthalin can provide to Morytania?

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Hold your disrespectful tongue! How dare you address the king in that way? We should throw you in the stocks!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I am sorry, but Aeonisig is my advisor, and what he says, while it pains me, is the truth. However, I will enact a mercenary protocol.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: This will allow all able-bodied men to fight in the defence of Misthalin for glory and spoils. You will find that many sword arms from Misthalin should soon join the resistance force.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: In the meantime, we will look into the Guthixian edicts and find a way to rid ourselves of Drakan for good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: That is for you to decide, but I would assume that you'd want to report back to your comrades in Morytania.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: If they're relying on support from Misthalin, you'll need to let them know that it might take some time before that happens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I came all this way for nothing?

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Is it nothing then to have an audience with the king? Your lack of respect astonishes me. How you have survived so long with such disgraceful manners is nothing short of a miracle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry. I had just hoped for so much more.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Well then, I must try to do something to satisfy you. I will have my advisor Aeonisig teleport you to Paterdomus so that you are more quickly on your way. Will you accept my offer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes thanks. I'll accept the free teleport.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Very good. My advisor, Aeonisig, will teleport you back. The best of luck to you.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Prepare yourself for teleport!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks. I've other things to do here.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Very well. Come back if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why can't you send troops to Morytania?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: The Guthixian edicts supposedly exist to ensure a balance is kept when it comes to religious and spiritual beliefs in the world. At the moment, things are considered to be in balance.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Misthalin is a Saradominist region, but Morytania is filled with creatures of Zamorak. A war between our two regions would be considered a war between Saradomin and Zamorak.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: If I send troops to Morytania, there is risk that the balance will be disrupted. Many followers of Guthix are apparently bound by oath to fight to keep the balance.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: It is said that when all three sides are joined in war, Guthix will 'reawaken and remake the world without mercy for the pain of beast or man.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that really true?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Personally, I don't think so. However, Aeonisig does not feel we should risk it until we can do further research on the Guthixian edicts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is a mercenary protocol?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: It means that people can fight with the blessings of the king in defence of the kingdom.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Normally, if a force is amassed, they may be considered a threat to Misthalin's national security. We'd have to send the army to disband them.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: However, a mercenary protocol allows a group to form a mercenary company. They are allowed certain rights so long as they operate within the law.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: The Guthixian edicts prevent us from doing too much at this point, but this situation in Morytania requires some action. Unfortunately, this is the best thing I can think of at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When can you send aid to Morytania?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: It depends on how long it takes the Church of Saradomin to read through the Guthixian edicts and return with an answer on how to proceed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What part does Guthix play in all this?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Guthixian edicts rule over those of all other religions. Many followers of Guthix claim that the edicts have largely controlled the peace that Misthalin has experienced for so many years.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Apparently, some followers of Guthix have sworn an oath to perform ceremonies to try and re-awaken Guthix if the edicts are broken.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: They say that peace and tolerance are needed to maintain the balance. I've no doubt it's all a load of rubbish, but we can't proceed until we are sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to King Roald again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does your offer of a teleport still stand?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Certainly, are you ready now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes thanks. I'll accept the free teleport.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Very good. My advisor, Aeonisig, will teleport you back. The best of luck to you.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Prepare yourself for teleport!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks. I've other things to do here.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Very well. Come back if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to ask a few questions if I may?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Certainly, go ahead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to King Roald after being teleported
Talking to Drezel after talking to King Roald

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. Have you been to Varrock yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have. King Roald isn't able to send troops for our fight against the Drakans. Apparently, some Guthixian edicts need to be scrutinised in more detail or else the balance will be affected?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Oh dear! I once did some studying of the edicts. I can see why the king and his advisors are cautious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The Myreque will need to know all that you have experienced so far. You should report to Veliaf when you can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you explain a little about the Guthixian edicts?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well, only a little. The followers of Guthix make all sorts of wild claims where he is involved. Those of us who serve Saradomin know that these claims are mostly baseless.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: What we do know is that Guthix is a god of nature and balance. We believe him to be of average power. Enough to pay attention to, but nothing compared to the strength of Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: It is said that long ago, back when the gods walked these lands with us, a great war was fought called the God Wars.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The stories say the war lasted for thousands of years and that the carnage saw the world forever changed.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The followers of Guthix claim that this war ended when Guthix awoke from his slumber and banished the other gods, leaving the world to mortals.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: This is likely not the true version of events. Guthixians also claim that it was Guthix himself who made the world. Obviously, that is not correct.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where do these edicts come in?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: They were apparently created at the end of the God Wars to ensure that the world never sees a conflict like that again.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The edicts state that no single god should be allowed to gain enough power to become dominant. Instead, a balance must be kept between the gods and their servants.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: If the balance is not kept, the followers of Guthix will try to intervene. If they are unable to restore balance, they will supposedly awaken Guthix from his slumber beneath the earth.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Once he awakens, they say he will remake the world, destroying civilisation as we know it.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: It's just a story of course. All made up by the Guthixians to help them feel relevant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if the edicts are just made up, what's the risk?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well when there's threats of the world being remade, you can't be too careful. It is wise to be cautious. After all, wisdom is one of Saradomin's greatest strengths.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Hiylik Myna

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me why you're here?

lines.speaker: Hiylik Myna
lines.text: Of course. I plan to assist the forces of Saradomin who will come to our aid, and fight against the legions of vampyres that infest this foul place. These brave mercenaries shall need a guide.

lines.speaker: Hiylik Myna
lines.text: When a guide arrives, I will aid the mercenary by holding their wealth for their eventual return. The mercenary will repay the guide by giving him a token for items which I am holding.

lines.speaker: Hiylik Myna
lines.text: I shall give such items to the guide as payment for their services once I'm handed the token.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What items will you hold?

lines.speaker: Hiylik Myna
lines.text: Oh, the range of items is very varied. Mercenaries tend to hold raw materials in high esteem and so will deposit those with me. They also seem to be good bartering items.

lines.speaker: Hiylik Myna
lines.text: Generally, things like uncooked lobsters, coal, silver bars, bow strings. Things which are useful! Plus the odd tome of experience, which are considered incredibly useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Vertida Sefalatis after talking to King Roald

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Myreque prevail, [player name]. You should have a chat with Safalaan. He may be able to help you.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf Hurtz after talking to King Roald

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met, friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Veliaf. I have some news for you.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was attacked near the temple! On the other side of the Salve!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Attacked by who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: By beasts! From Morytania, I believe. Perhaps werewolves.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The vampyres must be behind this! Did you speak with Roald yet? We need his support if we are to end this suffering.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Roald cannot send troops! I talked to him at great length and was unable to persuade him. His Saradominist advisor cited Guthixian edicts as the reason for not sending aid.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Guthixian edicts? How do they prevent Roald from sending troops?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently a war in Morytania will risk disturbing the balance between Saradomin and Zamorak so the Guthixians will need to intervene.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Church of Saradomin is looking into it now to see if it's true.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: There has long been a desire by the Church of Saradomin to see Guthixian power eroded away. This stinks of politics. They're just using the situation here for their own gain!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Roald did agree to enact a mercenary protocol. He said it was the best he could offer for the moment without contravening the Guthixian edicts.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well that's something I guess. Not exactly what we were hoping for though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should we do now? There must be someone else we can turn to?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: We must inform the Sanguinesti order of the Myreque of these events. Please go as fast as you can back to the Meiyerditch hideout and forward this information to them.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf again after being sent to inform the Sanguinesti Myreque

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met, friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do now?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: We must inform the Sanguinesti order of the Myreque of what we have learnt. Please go as fast as you can back to the Meiyerditch hideout and forward this information to them.

sections.title: Talking to Drezel after being sent to inform the Sanguinesti Myreque

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. How are things in Morytania?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not as well as I'd hope, Drezel. I'm still working with the Myreque. I just hope that we can find a way to defeat those Drakans.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: You can be sure that a great many people hope for exactly the same thing, [player name]. Good luck to you, and Myreque prevail!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Myreque prevail, Drezel.

sections.title: Close encounters with Vyre kind
Talking to Vertida Sefalatis after being sent to inform the Sanguinesti Myreque

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Myreque prevail! What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a message from Veliaf. It's bad news I'm afraid. I was sent on a mission to Misthalin to seek help from King Roald. Sadly, he isn't able to offer much support.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not all though. On my way to Varrock, I came across some beasts on the other side of the River Salve! I think they were werewolves working for the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: This is disastrous! Things seem to go from bad to worse! You will have to communicate this information to our leader, Safalaan. He needs to know as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Safalaan and what does he look like?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: He's out on a mission sketching Castle Drakan. He's taken the wall route so you'll need to do the same.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: There is a barricade that prevents further travel north. It's located under the wall. You'll need to search the wall southeast of the barricade to release the lock on the door.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, great, that's helpful. Now, how can I recognise Safalaan?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: He's very distinctive. It shouldn't be a problem trying to find him if you know where to look, and you've got a good set of eyes on you!

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Anyway, I need to head towards the wall myself now, so you can come with me. It'll be quicker than going back on your own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, lead the way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll make my own way.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: I'll be here if you change your mind.

sections.title: Talking to Vertida Sefalatis again after being sent to meet Safalaan

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. What do I need to do now?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: You need to report to our leader, Safalaan. He's out on a mission sketching Castle Drakan. He's taken the wall route so you'll need to do the same.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: There is a barricade that prevents further travel north. It's located under the wall. You'll need to search the wall southeast of the barricade to release the lock on the door.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, great, that's helpful. How can I recognise Safalaan?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: He's very distinctive. It shouldn't be a problem trying to find him if you know where to look, and you've got a good set of eyes on you!

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Anyway, I need to head towards the wall myself now, so you can come with me. It'll be quicker than going back on your own.

sections.title: Starting the route to the walls of Castle Drakan
Attempting to open the barricade with Vertida's advise
Searching the rocky surface with Vertida's advise
Talking to Safalaan Hallow

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You must be Safalaan. My name is [player name]. I come with a very important message from Vertida.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Vertida, eh? What makes you think I know a Vertida?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's asked me to come and seek you out. I'm working with Veliaf in Burgh de Rott.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Ah, Veliaf! Myreque prevail! My friend, you're just the tonic for my otherwise dark mood. Tell me, what news comes from the west?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I went to see King Roald to request support in our fight. Unfortunately, his advisor reminded him that the Guthixian edicts prevent his intervention.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Guthixian edicts? Sounds like excuses to me. I commend you on your efforts to rally support for our cause though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid that's not all. I was attacked by beasts on the western side of the Salve, near to Paterdomus. I think they were werewolves working for the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: This is terrible! Surely the magical influence of the Salve should be preventing that? What if the vampyres are able to cross as well? Drakan could start a full onslaught!

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I must return to the base in order to discuss this information and work out what must be done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help in some way?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Yes, you certainly can, my friend. I was on a mission to scout out this castle. I have yet to start, but please could you take over for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can certainly try! Are there any specific details I should know?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Indeed there are. We need sketches of the northern, western, and southern sides of the castle.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I've checked the vyrewatch flight patterns and I think you'll attract less attention if you make your way to the north and do that sketch first, then sketch the western side, and finally finish off here.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: One of the others has already marked the best spots to sketch from. If you need some more papyrus, you can get some off Old Man Ral. He's helped us out once or twice before.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Good luck, my friend. Return to the hideout when you have completed the sketches.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan Hallow again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Myreque prevail!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to do again?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: We need some accurate plans of the castle. Please make some careful sketches starting from the north side, moving to the west and finishing off with a sketch from this view.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: One of the others has already placed some markers on the walls at the best vantage points.

sections.title: Talking to Vertida Sefalatis after talking to Safalaan Hallow

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Myreque prevail, [player name]. You should have a chat with Safalaan. He may be able to help you.

sections.title: Talking to Old Man Ral after talking to Safalaan Hallow

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safalaan said I could get some more papyrus from you.

lines.speaker: Old Man Ral
lines.text: Hmph, hmm, I suppose so, here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks

lines.text: Well met, [player name]. How goes it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Veliaf. I'm doing some work for the Sanguinesti Myreque.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Good. It seems like you're really proving your worth. Keep it up.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: We really need those sketches showing the plans of the castle. Use charcoal and papyrus in order to make the sketches. There are three sides to the castle that need to be sketched.

lines.text: I need to make a sketch of the western face of the castle.

sections.title: Sketching the west side

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: So yes, Lady Vanescula, we're getting maximum yield from the current rotation. And more tithes are coming in all the time.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Good, good! You seem to be well in control.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: If I remember, madam, I had little choice. You were quite... 'persuasive' the last time we met.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Ha, ha, ha. Vanstrom, you do make me laugh.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Perhaps a light refreshment m'lady? This is from the fresh batch.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Why, with these blood tithes you're really spoiling us!

lines.speaker: Ranis Drakan
lines.text: Visciously efficient as ever, Vanstrom! You never fail to please us.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Mmm, delicious! Well, it's been lovely as usual, Vanstrom.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: But we really have to get on.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: The same is true for me, m'lady.

sections.title: Attempting to sketch the west side again
Using the castle sketch 1 or 2 on Safalaan before sketching the third

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Sorry, but that item doesn't look genuine. I cannot accept it.

sections.title: Sketching the south side

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Ha! So we meet again, [player name]. Prepare to die!

lines.speaker: Sarius Guile
lines.text: Sorry for the interruption, Lord Vanstrom, but Lady Vanescula 'requests' that this individual be questioned before being used as a punch bag.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I take no orders from food sacks like you, Guile. Attend to this yourself. In any case, [he/she] wasn't much of a punch bag anyway.

lines.speaker: Sarius Guile
lines.text: Awaken! There are things for you to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh? What happened?

lines.speaker: Sarius Guile
lines.text: Your life may be forfeit but for now you live. My mistress will understand my motives. She has an interest in the one called Safalaan.

lines.speaker: Sarius Guile
lines.text: As a gesture for my intervening on your behalf, I'd take it as a kindness if you would deliver a message to him.

lines.speaker: Sarius Guile
lines.text: The message you will find secreted behind tiles where smoke is carried away from the depiction of statuesque evil.

lines.speaker: Sarius Guile
lines.text: And now be away, but forget not the task that Safalaan set you! For why else would you be here?

sections.title: Sketching the south side after being saved by Sarius Guile
Attempting to sketch the south side again
Talking to Safalaan Hallow after being saved by Sarius Guile

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I've finished at the castle.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Good work. Do you have the sketches then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... No.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Sounds like you're not finished after all then. Please return once you have the sketches.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan Hallow with all three sketches

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I've finished at the castle. It was a close one though. I was attacked by Vanstrom!

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He came out of nowhere! I tried to fight back, but I just couldn't hurt him.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Brave, very brave. He didn't follow you here did he?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm pretty sure I wasn't followed. So anyway, during the fight, Vanstrom knocked me to the floor and I thought I was done for. But then a woman named Sarius showed up.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Sarius? Vanescula Drakan's servant? Hmmm, interesting. Was Vanescula herself nearby?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I saw Vanescula earlier, but when I saw Sarius arrive, Vanescula wasn't around. Sarius told Vanstrom that Vanescula wanted to interrogate me. So Vanstrom got angry and flew away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sarius then told me about a message which was meant for you. Something about 'smoke and statues'? She said that Vanescula was interested in you for some reason?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well, [player name], you certainly seem to have had a very close call. Thanks for letting me know all of this. It's all highly interesting.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Oh, and I almost forgot. Do you have the sketches?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You have my gratitude, friend! Myreque prevail! I need to take a good look at these and see what we can learn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Hmm, good question. Didn't you mention something about Sarius and a message?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. If I remember correctly, she said it was hidden 'behind tiles where smoke is carried away from the depiction of statuesque evil'.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: A riddle? Clearly she doesn't want the message to be easily found. I'd appreciate it if you could look into this further.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You've done well, [player name], very well indeed. I can't decipher much of it, but from what I can tell, this tome contains research notes on the vampyres' weaknesses.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, whatever it is, someone went to a lot of trouble to try and hide it.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I can believe that, [player name]. Once again you have our gratitude. For my part, your arrival has not come soon enough. I have a feeling that your efforts in our struggle are not yet over.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I for one would be grateful for your continued assistance. Together we can pull back the darkness of Hallowvale.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do what I can, Safalaan. You have my support.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Then I must beg you for one more small task, to return to Veliaf with a message to advise him of what has transpired here.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I realise that bearing messages seems somewhat lowly, but your ability to pass in and out of the city without being detected makes you perfect for this assignment.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I also have some information which should help with your journeys through Meiyerditch. There are two doors, one is in sector one and the other is in sector two. You can use them to reach this hideout faster.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, I'll return to Veliaf. Farewell.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Farewell and good luck, my friend!

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan Hallow after receiving the sealed message

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do again?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Take the message that I gave you to Veliaf. He should be able to explain everything to you when you get there.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan Hallow after losing the sealed message

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do again?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Take the message that I gave you to Veliaf. He should be able to explain everything to you when you get there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I've lost that message. Can I have another one please?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well, okay, but please don't lose this one.

sections.title: Attempting to read the sealed message
Talking to Veliaf Hurtz without the message from Safalaan Hallow

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Veliaf. I have some news for you.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met, [player name]. It's good to see you again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've spoken to Safalaan. He gave me a message to give to you but I've misplaced it!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well, that's not particularly good is it? You'll have to go back and ask him if he can rewrite the message for you.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf Hurtz with the message from Safalaan Hallow

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Veliaf. I have some news for you.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met, [player name]. It's good to see you again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a message for you from Safalaan.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Really? Well, let's see it then!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well, it seems that you have more than proven yourself worthy, [player name]. Safalaan himself has recommended you for a promotion to the rank of a private within the Myreque!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You've done well. I'd say you've earnt a break. Before you go though, here's a little something to thank you for your hard work.

sections.title: Post quest
Talking to Veliaf Hurtz after finishing the quest

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost my Tome of Experience.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Hmm, okay, let's see if I have another one for you... Ah, here we go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what's going to happen now?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: There's plenty of options for us to move forwards. We'll still keep up intelligence-gathering operations within the local area. We'll also keep looking for weaknesses in the vampyres' defences.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I'll probably have to meet up with Safalaan at some point, so we can discuss strategies. This war is far from over, [player name]. There's plenty of time to contribute so don't worry about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you just let me know when you next need me.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Don't worry, we will. Myreque prevail.
